extraction.one_line_summary: BARDET won from breakaway while FROOME survived mid-climb mechanical by deploying entire Sky team to close 40-second gap on final Cat-1 climb
tactical_patterns: AG2R attacked immediately during Froome's mechanical rather than waiting, forcing Sky to burn all domestiques on penultimate climb.
Landa dropping back from lead group to pace Froome confirms Sky's GC hierarchy despite earlier speculation about dual leadership.
Martin cooperated with dropped breakaway riders instead of solo bridging, gaining fifth overall by conserving energy for final kilometer.
Yates showed surprising late-stage strength in sprint despite being deep in third week, signaling potential Alps form.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: survived mechanical crisis but required complete team sacrifice on Cat-1 climb, exposing isolation vulnerability in mountains
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: definitively proved team loyalty by dropping from lead group to pace Froome across 40-second gap
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: riding into fifth overall at 1:12 despite crash injuries, showing tactical maturity by drafting breakaway remnants
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: attacking with enough power to require chase from lead GC group in third week suggests dangerous Alps form
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: AG2R capitalized on Froome mechanical by attacking immediately, showing ruthless team tactical discipline
